pairs.question: What is the full name and birthdate of the lead singer of Arctic Monkeys?
pairs.answer: The lead singer of Arctic Monkeys is Alexander David Turner, born on 6 January 1986.

pairs.question: What are the two most notable albums by Arctic Monkeys that appeared on Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Albums of All Time?
pairs.answer: The two most notable albums by Arctic Monkeys that appeared on Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Albums of All Time are 'Whatever People Say I Am, That's What I'm Not' (2006) and 'AM' (2013).

pairs.question: Name the two bands Alex Turner has co-founded and briefly describe their musical styles.
pairs.answer: Alex Turner co-founded Arctic Monkeys, known for experimenting with desert rock, indie pop, R&B, and lounge music. He also co-founded The Last Shadow Puppets, which released two orchestral pop albums: 'The Age of the Understatement' (2008) and 'Everything You've Come to Expect' (2016).

pairs.question: What was Alex Turner's solo debut, and in what year was it released?
pairs.answer: Alex Turner's solo debut was an extended play (EP) titled 'Submarine', released in 2010. This EP also served as the acoustic soundtrack for the feature film 'Submarine'.

pairs.question: Write a Python snippet to check if Alex Turner has won an Ivor Novello Award.
Why is Alex Turner's lyricism highly regarded by music critics?
pairs.answer: Alex Turner's lyricism is highly regarded by music critics due to its range and depth, spanning from 'kitchen sink realism' to 'surrealist wordplay', which adds unique artistic value to his music.
